slices.name: SecurityKernel
slices.code:
32 | class SecurityKernel:
  33 |     SENSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  34 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----.*?-----END[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----", re.DOTALL),
  35 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----.*?-----END CERTIFICATE-----", re.DOTALL),
  36 |         re.compile(r"(api_key|secret_key|auth_token)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}['\"]", re.IGNORECASE),
  37 |         re.compile(r"(password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][^'\"]{8,}['\"]", re.IGNORECASE),
  38 |     ]
  39 | 
  40 |     @staticmethod
  41 |     def is_binary(filepath: str, scan_bytes: int = 2048) -> bool:
  42 |         if any(filepath.lower().endswith(ext) for ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS):
  43 |             return True
  44 |         try:
  45 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as handle:
  46 |                 chunk = handle.read(scan_bytes)
  47 |             if not chunk:
  48 |                 return False
  49 |             if b'\x00' in chunk:
  50 |                 return True
  51 |             guess, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(filepath)
  52 |             if guess and not guess.startswith(('text', 'application')):
  53 |                 return True
  54 |             text_chars = bytearray({7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 27} | set(range(0x20, 0x7F)))
  55 |             nontext = sum(byte not in text_chars for byte in chunk)
  56 |             return (nontext / len(chunk)) > 0.40
  57 |         except Exception:
  58 |             return True
  59 | 
  60 |     @staticmethod
  61 |     def calculate_entropy(text: str) -> float:
  62 |         if not text:
  63 |             return 0.0
  64 |         entropy = 0.0
  65 |         for idx in range(256):
  66 |             p_x = float(text.count(chr(idx))) / len(text)
  67 |             if p_x > 0:
  68 |                 entropy -= p_x * math.log(p_x, 2)
  69 |         return entropy
  70 | 
  71 |     @classmethod
  72 |     def sanitize_content(cls, content: str) -> str:
  73 |         for pattern in cls.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
  74 |             content = pattern.sub("[REDACTED_SENSITIVE_PATTERN]", content)
  75 | 
  76 |         sanitized_lines: List[str] = []
  77 |         for line in content.splitlines():
  78 |             if any(key in line.lower() for key in ['api', 'key', 'secret', 'token', 'auth', 'password']):
  79 |                 if cls.calculate_entropy(line) > 4.5:
  80 |                     parts = line.split('=', 1)
  81 |                     prefix = parts[0] if len(parts) == 2 else line.split(':', 1)[0]
  82 |                     sanitized_lines.append(f"{prefix}= [REDACTED_HIGH_ENTROPY]")
  83 |                     continue
  84 |             sanitized_lines.append(line)
  85 |         return "\n".join(sanitized_lines)

  42 |         if any(filepath.lower().endswith(ext) for ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS):
  43 |             return True
  44 |         try:
  45 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as handle:
  46 |                 chunk = handle.read(scan_bytes)
  47 |             if not chunk:
  48 |                 return False
  49 |             if b'\x00' in chunk:
  50 |                 return True
  51 |             guess, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(filepath)
  52 |             if guess and not guess.startswith(('text', 'application')):
  53 |                 return True
  54 |             text_chars = bytearray({7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 27} | set(range(0x20, 0x7F)))
  55 |             nontext = sum(byte not in text_chars for byte in chunk)
  56 |             return (nontext / len(chunk)) > 0.40
  57 |         except Exception:
  58 |             return True

slices.name: calculate_entropy
slices.code:
61 |     def calculate_entropy(text: str) -> float:
  62 |         if not text:
  63 |             return 0.0
  64 |         entropy = 0.0
  65 |         for idx in range(256):
  66 |             p_x = float(text.count(chr(idx))) / len(text)
  67 |             if p_x > 0:
  68 |                 entropy -= p_x * math.log(p_x, 2)
  69 |         return entropy

slices.name: sanitize_content
slices.code:
72 |     def sanitize_content(cls, content: str) -> str:
  73 |         for pattern in cls.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
  74 |             content = pattern.sub("[REDACTED_SENSITIVE_PATTERN]", content)
  75 | 
  76 |         sanitized_lines: List[str] = []
  77 |         for line in content.splitlines():
  78 |             if any(key in line.lower() for key in ['api', 'key', 'secret', 'token', 'auth', 'password']):
  79 |                 if cls.calculate_entropy(line) > 4.5:
  80 |                     parts = line.split('=', 1)
  81 |                     prefix = parts[0] if len(parts) == 2 else line.split(':', 1)[0]
  82 |                     sanitized_lines.append(f"{prefix}= [REDACTED_HIGH_ENTROPY]")
  83 |                     continue
  84 |             sanitized_lines.append(line)
  85 |         return "\n".join(sanitized_lines)

slices.name: NotebookPackager
slices.code:
90 | class NotebookPackager:
  91 |     def __init__(self, target_dir: pathlib.Path):
  92 |         self.target_dir = target_dir.resolve()
  93 |         self.files_registry: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
  94 |         self.directories_registry: set = set()
  95 |         
  96 |     def _create_markdown_cell(self, text: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  97 |         return {
  98 |             "cell_type": "markdown",
  99 |             "metadata": {},
 100 |             "source": [line + "\n" for line in text.splitlines()]
 101 |         }
 102 | 
 103 |     def _create_code_cell(self, text: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 104 |         return {
 105 |             "cell_type": "code",
 106 |             "execution_count": None,
 107 |             "metadata": {},
 108 |             "outputs": [],
 109 |             "source": [line + "\n" for line in text.splitlines()]
 110 |         }
 111 | 
 112 |     def traverse_and_read(self):
 113 |         for root, dirs, files in os.walk(self.target_dir):
 114 |             dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in IGNORE_DIRS and not d.startswith('.')]
 115 |             
 116 |             for file_name in files:
 117 |                 if file_name.startswith('.'):
 118 |                     continue
 119 |                     
 120 |                 path_obj = pathlib.Path(root) / file_name
 121 |                 rel_path = path_obj.relative_to(self.target_dir).as_posix()
 122 |                 
 123 |                 parent_dir = path_obj.parent.relative_to(self.target_dir).as_posix()
 124 |                 if parent_dir != ".":
 125 |                     self.directories_registry.add(parent_dir)
 126 | 
 127 |                 if SecurityKernel.is_binary(str(path_obj)):
 128 |                     continue
 129 |                     
 130 |                 size = path_obj.stat().st_size
 131 |                 is_python = path_obj.suffix.lower() == '.py'
 132 |                 
 133 |                 # Smart Size Limiter: Unlimited for .py files, limited for .json and others
 134 |                 if not is_python and size > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES:
 135 |                     print(f"Skipping {rel_path} due to size limit ({size / 1_000_000:.2f} MB)")
 136 |                     continue
 137 |                     
 138 |                 try:
 139 |                     with open(path_obj, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
 140 |                         raw = handle.read()
 141 |                         
 142 |                     self.files_registry.append({
 143 |                         "path": rel_path,
 144 |                         "size_bytes": size,
 145 |                         "content": SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(raw)
 146 |                     })
 147 |                 except Exception as e:
 148 |                     print(f"Skipping {rel_path} due to read error: {e}")
 149 | 
 150 |     def generate_notebook(self) -> str:
 151 |         cells = []
 152 |         
 153 |         # 1. Title & Instruction Markdown
 154 |         timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
 155 |         intro_text = (
 156 |             f"# Self-Extracting Workspace Bundle: `{self.target_dir.name}`\n"
 157 |             f"**Generated:** {timestamp}\n\n"
 158 |             "**Instructions:**\n"
 159 |             "Run all cells in this notebook to recreate the directory structure "
 160 |             "and files in your current Colab/Jupyter environment. "
 161 |         )
 162 |         cells.append(self._create_markdown_cell(intro_text))
 163 |         
 164 |         # 2. Directory Setup Code Cell
 165 |         if self.directories_registry:
 166 |             dirs_list = ",\n    ".join([f'"{d}"' for d in sorted(self.directories_registry)])
 167 |             setup_code = (
 168 |                 "import os\n\n"
 169 |                 "directories_to_create = [\n"
 170 |                 f"    {dirs_list}\n"
 171 |                 "]\n\n"
 172 |                 "for d in directories_to_create:\n"
 173 |                 "    os.makedirs(d, exist_ok=True)\n"
 174 |                 "    print(f'Created directory: {d}')\n"
 175 |                 "print('Directory setup complete!')"
 176 |             )
 177 |             cells.append(self._create_markdown_cell("### Step 1: Create Directory Tree"))
 178 |             cells.append(self._create_code_cell(setup_code))
 179 | 
 180 |         # 3. File Write Cells
 181 |         cells.append(self._create_markdown_cell("### Step 2: Extract Files"))
 182 |         
 183 |         has_requirements = False
 184 |         for file_meta in sorted(self.files_registry, key=lambda x: x['path']):
 185 |             # Check if this file is requirements.txt (in the root folder)
 186 |             if file_meta['path'].lower() == 'requirements.txt':
 187 |                 has_requirements = True
 188 |                 
 189 |             write_code = f"%%writefile {file_meta['path']}\n{file_meta['content']}"
 190 |             cells.append(self._create_markdown_cell(f"**Extracting:** `{file_meta['path']}` ({file_meta['size_bytes']} bytes)"))
 191 |             cells.append(self._create_code_cell(write_code))
 192 | 
 193 |         # 4. Install Dependencies (If requirements.txt exists)
 194 |         if has_requirements:
 195 |             cells.append(self._create_markdown_cell("### Step 3: Install Dependencies\nAutomatically installing packages from `requirements.txt`."))
 196 |             cells.append(self._create_code_cell("!pip install -r requirements.txt"))
 197 | 
 198 |         # Build final Notebook dictionary
 199 |         notebook_dict = {
 200 |             "cells": cells,
 201 |             "metadata": {
 202 |                 "language_info": {
 203 |                     "name": "python"
 204 |                 }
 205 |             },
 206 |             "nbformat": 4,
 207 |             "nbformat_minor": 5
 208 |         }
 209 |         
 210 |         return json.dumps(notebook_dict, indent=2)

 191 |             cells.append(self._create_code_cell(write_code))

 196 |             cells.append(self._create_code_cell("!pip install -r requirements.txt"))

slices.name: main
slices.code:
212 | def main():
 213 |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Jupyter Notebook Packager (Self-Extracting Bundle)")
 214 |     parser.add_argument("path", nargs="?", default=".", help="Project directory to package")
 215 |     parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", help="Output .ipynb filename")
 216 |     args = parser.parse_args()
 217 | 
 218 |     target_dir = pathlib.Path(args.path).resolve()
 219 |     if not target_dir.exists() or not target_dir.is_dir():
 220 |         sys.exit(f"Error: Target must be a valid directory: {target_dir}")
 221 | 
 222 |     print(f"Scanning directory: {target_dir}")
 223 |     packager = NotebookPackager(target_dir)
 224 |     packager.traverse_and_read()
 225 |     
 226 |     nb_content = packager.generate_notebook()
 227 | 
 228 |     if args.output:
 229 |         out_path = pathlib.Path(args.output)
 230 |         if out_path.suffix != '.ipynb':
 231 |             out_path = out_path.with_suffix('.ipynb')
 232 |     else:
 233 |         ts = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
 234 |         out_path = target_dir.parent / f"{target_dir.name}_bundle_{ts}.ipynb"
 235 | 
 236 |     try:
 237 |         with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 238 |             f.write(nb_content)
 239 |         print(f"\n[Success] Bundled {len(packager.files_registry)} files.")
 240 |         print(f"Notebook saved to: {out_path}")
 241 |     except Exception as e:
 242 |         sys.exit(f"Error writing output: {e}")
